Stone Energy successfully completed sand control operations on EI 243, OCS-G-02899, Well D-1ST in the "OW" Sand completion.  Operations began on February 6, 2009 and were completed on March 6, 2009.  Stone was able to remove a wireline fish in the hole, left during previous operations, and to perform a coil tubing clean out of the well.  Stone also installed a thru-tubing gravel pack assembly @ 12396' MD to reduce sand production from the "OW" sand completion interval.

BUS_ASC_NAME: Black Elk Energy Offshore Operations, LLC
OPERATIONAL_NARRATIVE: Recomplete to the L-5 sand

The MC 109 A28 well abandonment was completed and the well prepared for sidetrack operations.  On 12-14-10, the rig, H&P 206 skid on the well.  4 1/2" prod. tbg was cut & pulled @ 4563'.  A EZSV w/drill guns was set @ 4200'.  The drill guns were fired and the 9 5/8" csg was perforated.  150 sxs (163 cu ft) of cmt was squeezed into the 
13 3/8" x 9 5/8" annulus, w/ 30 sxs (32 cu ft) of cmt dumped on top of EZSV, w/ETOC in 9 5/8" csg @ 4110'.  The cmt plug @ 4110' was washed & reamed to 4150 ' & then tested to 3500 psi  w/10.5 ppg mud.    Operations were completed on 12-23-10, when the well was sidetracked, by cutting a window in the 9 5/8" csg @ 4101' MD.
Please note that 7" production casing was cut and pulled from 319'below the mudline 02/02/2011.
